description: baseline run after reset: startup measured but plugin_count probe returned empty in non-tty command substitution; fixing harness output
lazy-load heavy kickstart modules and drop custom import/todo-comments for minimal nvim profile; startup now 18.045ms with 20 plugins
remove tokyonight plugin/theme for lean default colors; startup improved to 16.134ms with 19 plugins
defer which-key load from VimEnter to VeryLazy; startup improved to 14.603ms while retaining key-hint functionality
lazy-load guess-indent on buffer events; measured 16.022ms so no gain over current profile
disable several builtin runtime plugins in init.lua; startup 15.026ms (slightly worse), so reverted
retest removing guess-indent under latest baseline; plugin count 18 but startup regressed to 15.773ms
remove which-key from minimal profile entirely; startup improved to 14.234ms and plugin count dropped to 18
remove guess-indent after dropping which-key; startup regressed to 17.29ms despite plugin count 17
remove mini.nvim from plugin set; startup regressed to 14.935ms so kept mini for UX
remove conform from minimal plugin set; startup improved to 13.769ms with 17 plugins
startup improved slightly without gitsigns (13.651ms) but rejected: git gutter feedback is part of required minimal UX
enable vim.loader explicitly in init.lua; no improvement (13.813ms), reverted
rewrite lazy-plugins.lua to compact minimal file; no startup gain (15.904ms), reverted to previous structure
stricter mirror/doc checks surfaced 3 unresolved drift items; experimental criteria expanded but no stable fix landed in this iteration
steady-state check of current minimal profile; startup 13.915ms, no improvement over best 13.769ms
compact rewrite of telescope plugin module increased startup to 15.138ms; revert and keep prior telescope file
remove lazydev integration from lsp/blink to cut one plugin; startup 13.805ms (slightly worse), reverted
defer guess-indent to BufReadPost/BufNewFile to avoid startup load; startup regressed to 14.849ms despite plugin_count 16
